html:
Exclusive to:

This article is incomplete because of the evil sabotage by Maxwell....
Please help to improve the article, or discuss the issue with the community on the talk page according to the talk page etiquette or on our Discord.
For the Don't Starve Together items of same name (Seed Pods/Garlic Seeds), see Crop Seeds.

A Seed Pod is a Food Item exclusive to the Hamlet DLC, which drops from Tea Trees, and which can be replanted to grow another one.

Eating a Seed Pod can temporarily negate the effects of Hay Fever for 60 seconds.

Seed Pod and Cooked Seed Pod cannot be used in a Crock Pot.

Cooked Seed Pod

Cooked Seed Pod is a Food item made by Cooking a Seed Pod on a Campfire, Fire Pit, or other viable source of fire.

Cooked Seed Pods have better restoring values than its raw counterpart, as it restores 10 hunger and 3 health, and will prevent sneezing for 120 seconds (instead of 60) when eaten. However, its spoilage time is much shorter (6 days).

Tips

  • Wigfrid is currently unable to eat Seed Pods, so the player will want to have a Gas Mask prepared before the Lush Season begins to avoid hay fever. Wormwood is immune to Hay Fever, so he doesn't need to eat Seed Pod.
  • Utilizing nothing but raw Seed Pods the player will need to amass 96 of them to sustain each Lush Season. In actual fact this number is slightly lower due to the slight delay of Hay Fever's effects.
  • Utilizing nothing but cooked Seed Pods the player will need to amass 48 of them to sustain each Lush Season. In actual fact this number is slightly lower due to the slight delay of Hay Fever's effects.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

"teatree_nut"
“Stops the seasonal sneezing.”
–Wilson
“I'd burn it but it helps my allergies.”
–Willow
“Is good for Wolfgang's mighty sneezes.”
–Wolfgang
“Makes life barely more livable in allergy season.”
–Wendy
“MULTI-USE ENERGY CONTAINER”
–WX-78
“A good antihistamine.”
–Wickerbottom
“Helps with allergies, eh.”
–Woodie
“I'll need this for hayfever season.”
–Maxwell
“My stomach condition prevents me from partaking in its health benefits.”
–Wagstaff
“Its lack öf meat öffends me.”
–Wigfrid
“It helps with our sneezes.”
–Webber
“I heard it's good for allergies.”
–Walani
“More useful as medicine than in my recipe.”
–Warly
“A botan'cal barber-surgeon.”
–Woodlegs
“TO EAT OR NOT TO EAT”
–Wilba
“Want in belly? Or dirt?”
–Wormwood
“Good enough to eat. Or fire out the Pew-matic.”
–Wheeler

“Take that, tiny tree!”
–Wilson
“I set it on fire so now I can eat it.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang eat tiny tree baby.”
–Wolfgang
“I killed it to prolong my own life.”
–Wendy
“BIOLOGICAL ENERGY PACKET”
–WX-78
“I'd prefer a proper cup of tea.”
–Wickerbottom
“Too late to plant this now.”
–Woodie
“I'll eat it but I won't like it.”
–Maxwell
“Of a chewy consistency. Evidently, it's good for you.”
–Wagstaff
“Even touched by fire, I will not consume it!”
–Wigfrid
“Yummy.”
–Webber
“A good meal for little effort.”
–Walani
“Not the finest of fine foods.”
–Warly
“Grub.”
–Woodlegs
“FWOOSH'DED NUT O' THE SQUIRRELY TREE”
–Wilba
“Not for dirt now”
–Wormwood
“For chewing or pew-ing.”
–Wheeler
Cooked Seed Pod is a Food item made by Cooking a Seed Pod on a Campfire, Fire Pit, or other viable source of fire.
Cooked Seed Pods have better restoring values than its raw counterpart, as it restores 10 hunger and 3 health, and will prevent sneezing for 120 seconds (instead of 60) when eaten. However, its spoilage time is much shorter (6 days).

Wigfrid is currently unable to eat Seed Pods, so the player will want to have a Gas Mask prepared before the Lush Season begins to avoid hay fever. Wormwood is immune to Hay Fever, so he doesn't need to eat Seed Pod.
Utilizing nothing but raw Seed Pods the player will need to amass 96 of them to sustain each Lush Season. In actual fact this number is slightly lower due to the slight delay of Hay Fever's effects.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Vỏ Hạt Giống là một Vật Phẩm Thực Phẩm dành riêng cho Hamlet DLC, rơi ra từ Cây Trà và có thể trồng lại để trồng một cây khác.
